title: AIAA-2006-544-851
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text and diagrams:

**1. Mathematical equations:**
- Pressure drop of 4.0% across the swirler
- MR = 0.0 (baseline case)
- MR = 0.07 (lowest mass ratio considered)
- MR = 0.1

**2. Graphs/charts:**
- Figure III.1: Axial velocity contours and velocity vectors for the baseline case (MR=0.0)
 * Axes: Vertical axis labeled "Z (mm)" from 10 to 120

**3. Tables:**
- Text refers to Table 5.5 contains test parameter data. Specific table is not shown.

**4. Diagrams:**
- Figure III.1:  Shows a diagram of axial velcity controus and velocity vectors through an axial slice of what appears to be the combustion chamber. There appears to be fuel nozzle and jet locations near the bottom.

**5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:**

- Mass ratio (MR) - varied from 0.07 to 0.4
- Jet deflection angle ≈ 50° (at MR = 0.07)
- Jet deflection angle reduces to 40° (at MR = 0.1)
- Recirculation zone length: extended to Z = 104 mm (baseline); extended to 112 mm.
- Recirculation zone size: occupies 45% of the chamber size at about Z = 35 mm (baseline)
- Swirler exit plane: Primary air injection 25 mm downstream
- Recirculation zone width: shortens from 55 mm to 42 mm at Z = 45 mm; reduces to 31 mm.
- Recirculation height extends to 118 mm

title: AIAA-2006-544-851
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical equations:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**2. Graphs/charts:** NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

*   **Table III.1 Influence of jet mass ratio on recirculation zone properties for primary air placed 25mm above the swirler exit plane**

    | Mass Ratio           | 0    | 0.07 | 0.1  | 0.15 | 0.2  | 0.3 | 0.4 |
    | -------------------- | ---- | ---- | ---- | ---- | ---- | --- | --- |
    | Deflection angle (deg.)| -    | 50   | 40   | 30   | 18   | 9   | 6   |
    | R.Z. width @ Z=45mm| 55   | 42   | 31   | 20   | -    | -   | -   |
    | R.Z. height (mm)     | 104  | 110  | 118  | 119  | 41.5 | 35  | 33  |

*   **Table III.2 Deflection angle at different primary air injection locations**

    | Mass Ratio | Z =25 mm | Z =50 mm | Z =76 mm |
    | ---------- | -------- | -------- | -------- |
    | 0.07       | 50       | 22       | 35       |
    | 0.1        | 40       | 18       | 10       |
    | 0.15       | 30       | 8        | 3        |
    | 0.2        | 18       | 3        | 1        |
    | 0.3        | 9        | 0        | 0        |
    | 0.4        | 6        | 0        | 0        |

**4. Diagrams:**NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Jet deflection angle reduced to 30 degrees
*   Z = 25 mm, Z = 50 mm, Z = 76 mm
*   Optimum mass ratio = 0.15
*   MR > 0.2
